title: seseli gummiferum subsp. aegaeum
intro: Seseli gummiferum subsp. aegaeum, also known as Aegaeum seseli, is a plant species native to Greece and Turkey. It is a type of umbelliferous plant that belongs to the carrot family. Traditionally, Aegaeum seseli has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The plant is used to make herbal preparations such as essential oils and teas, which are said to offer health benefits such as relieving digestive issues and reducing inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
